source.citation: F. Teppe et al., Nature Communications 7, 12576 (2016)

latent_gap_model.name: reconstructed Laurenti 1990 equation
latent_gap_model.role: near-critical local comparator, not a universally selected material law

authorized_conclusions: Near the nominal Teppe sample-B transition, composition variation is strongly amplified into local signed-gap and critical-temperature width.
A composition standard deviation of 0.001 corresponds to a first-order local gap scale of about 1.72 meV and critical-temperature scale of about 4.46 K under the reconstructed Laurenti law.
A composition standard deviation of 0.005 corresponds to about 8.60 meV and 22.32 K under the same local model.
The screen defines a precision requirement for composition-tuned transition claims.
unauthorized_conclusions: The Teppe specimen is not claimed to have either composition standard deviation.
The propagated gap standard deviation is not identified with an optical Urbach energy, PL FWHM, or quasiparticle linewidth.
The local opposite-sign fraction is not a bulk topological invariant.
The Laurenti equation is not selected as a universal HgCdTe gap law by this screen.
